name: Erbil
summary: Erbil, capital of the Kurdistan Region, is significantly safer than other parts of Iraq but requires caution due to regional tensions. While crime rates are low and locals are exceptionally hospitable, the city operates under a Level 4 US travel advisory due to broader security concerns.
quickVerdict: Despite being part of Iraq's Level 4 Do Not Travel advisory, Erbil offers a relatively safe experience within the Kurdistan Region's autonomous territory. The city has remarkably low crime rates, with visitors regularly noting excellent hospitality and safety during daylight hours. However, the ongoing regional tensions, recent missile strikes, and proximity to conflict zones require careful planning and awareness of the evolving security situation.

neighborhoods.name: Erbil Citadel & Downtown
neighborhoods.description: The historic heart of the city centered around the ancient citadel and surrounding bazaars. Well-patrolled tourist area with good safety infrastructure, though some tourist-targeted price inflation occurs. Safe for walking during day and early evening.

neighborhoods.name: Ankawa District
neighborhoods.description: Christian-majority neighborhood known for its restaurants, cafes, and nightlife venues. Generally considered one of the safest areas in Erbil with a more liberal atmosphere. Popular with expats and international visitors.

neighborhoods.name: City Center & Business District
neighborhoods.description: Modern commercial area with shopping centers, hotels, and business facilities. Well-developed infrastructure with good security presence. Heavy traffic during peak hours but generally safe for visitors.

neighborhoods.name: Sami Abdulrahman Park Area
neighborhoods.description: Large park and recreational area popular with families. Safe during daylight hours with regular security patrols. Exercise standard precautions after dark as lighting can be inconsistent in some areas.

neighborhoods.name: Outer Suburbs
neighborhoods.description: Residential areas extending toward city limits. Generally safe but with less tourist infrastructure and occasional checkpoints. Recommended to travel with local knowledge or guides when exploring these areas.

neighborhoods.name: Border Areas
neighborhoods.description: Areas near Turkish and Iranian borders where military operations against PKK occur regularly. Frequent airstrikes, security operations, and restricted access zones make these areas dangerous for civilians and strictly off-limits to tourists.

scams.name: Tourist Price Inflation
scams.description: Vendors in tourist areas, particularly around the Citadel and bazaars, may charge inflated prices to foreigners. Coffee shops and restaurants might have different pricing for tourists versus locals.
scams.howToAvoid: Always ask for prices upfront, negotiate firmly but politely, and observe what locals pay. Consider having a local companion when shopping for significant purchases.

scams.name: Unofficial Taxi Overcharging
scams.description: Unofficial taxi drivers may charge excessive fares to tourists or take unnecessarily long routes. Some may pose as official app-based drivers when they are not.
scams.howToAvoid: Use only Careem app for taxi services, which provides standard pricing and driver verification. Always verify license plate numbers match your booking before entering the vehicle.

scams.name: Accommodation Double-Booking
scams.description: Some hotels or guesthouses may claim bookings are invalid or demand additional payments upon arrival, particularly during peak periods or events.
scams.howToAvoid: Book through reputable platforms, keep confirmation emails accessible, and call ahead to confirm reservations. Consider staying in established international hotel chains.

scams.name: Fake Government Services
scams.description: Fraudulent companies claiming government contracts or offering visa services through unofficial channels. Some fake websites impersonate official government services.
scams.howToAvoid: Use only official government websites and embassies for visa services. Be suspicious of unsolicited business offers and verify any official claims through proper channels.

soloFemale.overview: Solo female travel in Erbil is possible but requires extra precautions and cultural sensitivity. While the Kurdistan Region is more liberal than other parts of Iraq, conservative Islamic values still predominate. Women report feeling generally safe during daylight hours but recommend traveling with companions when possible.
tips: Dress modestly covering shoulders, arms, and legs; headscarf recommended for religious sites
Stay in well-reviewed accommodations in central areas like Ankawa or downtown
Use Careem app for transport rather than street taxis for added safety and accountability
Avoid isolated areas after dark and stick to well-lit, populated streets in the evening
Connect with other female travelers or locals through social media groups for support and companionship

nightSafety.overview: Nighttime safety in Erbil varies significantly by area and current security conditions. While violent crime is rare, the combination of regional tensions, occasional security incidents, and limited emergency services after dark requires extra caution. Most locals recommend avoiding unnecessary travel after 10 PM.
tips: Stay in well-lit central areas like Ankawa which has better nightlife infrastructure
Use Careem app for transportation rather than walking at night
Keep updated on current security situation through embassy alerts and local news
Inform someone of your whereabouts and expected return time
Have emergency contacts readily available including hotel, consulate, and local emergency numbers

transport.metro: Erbil has no metro or formal public transit system. The city relies entirely on private vehicles, taxis, and ride-hailing services for urban transport.
transport.rideshare: Careem is the primary ride-hailing service operating reliably in Erbil with standardized pricing and driver verification. The app works well throughout the city and provides safer transport than unofficial taxis.
transport.taxis: Two types of official taxis operate: orange and white city taxis, and tan government-vetted taxis (considered safest). Unofficial taxis should be avoided due to safety and pricing concerns.
transport.tips: Always use Careem app for ride requests, download Arabic numeral translation images to match license plates, avoid unmarked private cars, and expect traffic congestion during peak hours.

customs: Remove shoes when entering homes and wait to be seated by your host
Accept offers of tea or coffee as refusing hospitality can be considered impolite
Use your right hand for eating, greeting, and passing items as the left hand is considered unclean
Dress modestly with covered shoulders and legs, especially when visiting religious sites
Show respect for elders by greeting them first and offering your seat if needed

health.overview: Erbil has several quality private hospitals with international-trained doctors, but public healthcare infrastructure is limited. Medical facilities are adequate for routine care but serious medical emergencies may require evacuation to neighboring countries.
health.water: Do not drink tap water. Use bottled water for drinking and teeth brushing. Tap water may be used for cooking hot foods but bottled water recommended for cold preparations and ice.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required beyond routine immunizations. Consider hepatitis A/B and typhoid for extended stays. Yellow fever certificate required if arriving from affected areas.
health.altitude: Erbil sits at 400m elevation with hot, dry summers reaching 45°C and mild winters. Air quality can be poor due to dust and limited emissions controls. High UV exposure requires sun protection.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Consulate General Erbil: +964-66-211-4554 (office hours), +964-66-211-4000 (after hours emergency). Due to current Level 4 advisory, consular services are extremely limited and emergency assistance capabilities are restricted.

faq.a: Erbil is relatively safe within the Kurdistan Region context, with low crime rates and hospitable locals. However, it operates under a US Level 4 Do Not Travel advisory due to regional security concerns including terrorist threats and cross-border conflicts. Tourist infrastructure exists but requires careful planning.

Nighttime safety is limited in Erbil. While violent crime is rare, regional security concerns and limited emergency services make evening activities risky. Stay in central areas like Ankawa, use Careem for transport, and avoid unnecessary travel after 10 PM.

faq.q: Is Erbil safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Solo female travel is possible but challenging in Erbil. While harassment is not common, conservative cultural norms and regional security issues require extra precautions. Dress modestly, use trusted accommodations, travel during daylight when possible, and consider connecting with other travelers.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Erbil?
faq.a: Avoid border areas with Turkey and Iran due to ongoing military operations against PKK. Stay away from government buildings, military installations, and minimize time near oil infrastructure. Monitor current security alerts for temporary restricted areas.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Erbil?
faq.a: Erbil has no formal public transit. Use Careem ride-hailing app for safe, standardized transport. Avoid unofficial taxis and unmarked vehicles. Government-vetted tan taxis are safer than orange/white city taxis if official ride-hailing is unavailable.
